introParagraph: The capital of Romania, Bucharest, once a disorganised and rather oriental city, became, by the turn of the 20th century, a modern European city heavily inspired by French architecture and planning. This led to the city gaining the nickname of 'Paris of the East' or 'Little Paris'.
introParagraph2: During the Interwar period, Bucharest saw a flourishing of the arts and culture alongside great development in its economy and social life. In Interwar Romania, Bucharest set the standard and all of the latest fashion trends and technological advancements reached the capital first. From some of the most beautiful buildings and avenues, to the most chic parlors, entertaining cultural spots and newest movies, Bucharest had the best the country could offer.
introParagraph3: While the age of jazz and the likes of cabaret and charleston dancers have long departed from the streets of Bucharest and its Interwar charm has faded over time, archival images, magazines and other items from the era can still paint a picture of what once was in the capital of Romania. If you look closely, you can still find glimpses of its former glory today, so take a chance and explore Interwar Bucharest!
